title: Son of a Witch
anchor_summary: Co-op fantasy action roguelike with procedurally generated dungeon runs, class-based heroes, heavy item/weapon synergies, and brawler/shooter combat for solo or up to four players.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently identify this as an 'action roguelike for 1-4 players' with procedurally generated dungeons, permadeath, random encounters, many bosses, item/weapon synergies, and single-player plus up to 4-player co-op. The core loop is run-based dungeon combat with repeated fresh worlds, boss fights, and build variation from weapons, potions, scrolls, pets, and heroes. That strongly fits co_op_action_roguelite. PvP is listed in Steam categories, but the textual gameplay identity is overwhelmingly co-op roguelite action rather than a match-based PvP game.

title: Mordheim: City of the Damned - Witch Hunters
anchor_summary: A faction DLC for Mordheim: City of the Damned that keeps the base game's core identity: turn-based tactical warband skirmishes, squad building, campaign progression, and equipment/skill customization in a grim fantasy setting.

similar_games.title: Mordheim: City of the Damned
similar_games.why_similar: This DLC directly inherits the base game's turn-based warband tactics, campaign structure, roster management, and progression systems.

similar_games.title: Necromunda: Underhive Wars
similar_games.why_similar: Another Games Workshop skirmish tactics game built around small-gang battles, persistent unit progression, and equipment-driven customization.

similar_games.title: Battle Brothers
similar_games.why_similar: Focuses on managing a persistent roster through punishing tactical encounters, with injuries, gear choices, and long-term squad growth.

similar_games.title: XCOM 2
similar_games.why_similar: Shares turn-based squad combat, soldier development, ability builds, and high-stakes tactical decision-making across a campaign.

similar_games.title: Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate - Daemonhunters
similar_games.why_similar: A Warhammer turn-based tactics campaign centered on customizable squads, mission-based progression, and ability-focused combat.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: The stored evidence clearly indicates this is DLC content for Mordheim: City of the Damned and describes additive warband/campaign content, unit types, weapons, perks, and spells. It also supports a strategy/RPG identity and tactical optimization themes, but it does not provide enough concrete core-loop evidence to safely map the gameplay to a single taxonomy archetype from the allowed list. Critical genre-defining details such as turn structure, camera/perspective, party-control model, battle format, and exploration structure are not established strongly enough from the DLC text alone, and no parent-game evidence is supplied for inheritance.
